title: acceptEdits does NOT pre-approve .vscode writes -> the protected path still prompts (ask); same rule covers .idea/.husky/.cargo/… and protected files

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/P5-protected-paths/d-vscode-protected/.vscode/PROBE.txt with the content 'x'. Do it immediately. Use only the Write tool — if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.
probes.result_text: The Write tool call was blocked — the path `$REPO/cases/P5-protected-paths/d-vscode-protected/.vscode/PROBE.txt` is flagged as a sensitive file and requires explicit permission to edit.

hypothesis: The official protected-directory list is .git, .config/git, .vscode, .idea, .husky, .cargo, .devcontainer, .yarn, .mvn, .claude (except .claude/worktrees), plus a protected-FILE list (.gitconfig, .npmrc, .mcp.json, .claude.json, shell rc files, …). All are 'Prompted' in default/acceptEdits/plan (engine=ask, approvable), never auto-approved. .vscode stands in for the whole class here; the mechanism is identical to .git (P5-a) and .claude (P5-c). Headless auto-denies the ask; SDK canUseTool fires (ASK).
